behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: failure boundary is parser -> hook-input handling handoff, not just one pure function (MOCK-MISS). No required real OS/network/subprocess is stated, so System is not required (REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI output dependency (b=no) and no multi-agent chain (c=no). PASS2 counter-argument down to Unit rejected by behavior text "parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling" (explicit cross-module flow). Counter-argument up to System rejected by absence of "real process/stdin/subprocess" language here.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real parser + hook-input handler in-process; send valid JSON whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse success and downstream handler receives expected structured payload without parse-failure trace.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: needs parser error + trace-record construction together (module interaction). A unit test of parser alone would miss trace-context mapping. No real process requirement in this behavior text, so System not mandatory (a=no). No LLM dependency (b=no), no agentic chain (c=no). PASS2 down-level Unit rejected by phrase "produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record" (crosses parser and tracing). Up-level System rejected because "real process execution" is not part of this behavior (that appears in behavior 4).
behaviors.test_description: Run malformed JSON through in-process hook input pipeline (parser + trace sink adapter); assert one `json_parse_failed` record is emitted and includes diagnostic context (e.g., error offset/snippet/path) sufficient to identify malformed segment.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: behavior is explicitly "local error-policy path" mapping one error input to one handled output + telemetry-intent flag; can be validated at one object/function boundary with no I/O (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor fits). Integration-BRAKE checks: no OS/network/subprocess (a=no), no AI output dependence (b=no), no multi-agent chain (c=no). PASS2 up-level Integration rejected by concrete text "local error-policy path" which disqualifies need for module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke error-policy mapper directly with a parse-failure error object; assert it returns handled/non-throw result and sets telemetry-intent marker in returned policy decision.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin handling, which depends on subprocess/stdin/exit behavior (REAL-INFRA yes; MOCK-HIDE yes if faked). Integration is too low because in-process fakes can miss crash/exit-order issues. No LLM dependency or multi-agent sequence. PASS2 down-level Integration rejected by concrete phrase "real process execution" and "before exit" (OS process lifecycle boundary).
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as subprocess, pipe malformed bytes to stdin, wait for process exit; assert non-crash behavior (controlled exit/handled status) and verify trace signal is emitted before termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 System: behavior names a "real hook invocation path" beginning at stdin, implying real invocation and I/O path. That is a subprocess/OS boundary, so System (REAL-INFRA yes). Integration could verify parser->sink wiring but misses real stdin/invocation edge behavior (MOCK-HIDE risk). No AI dependency or multi-agent chain. PASS2 down-level Integration rejected by concrete text "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)".
behaviors.test_description: Execute hook through its real entrypoint, send malformed input via stdin, and assert the real trace artifact (file/log/event sink) contains expected `json_parse_failed` signal and context fields.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: regression objective compares two end-to-end input classes across parser + error classification/trace behavior; boundary is module handoff, not single pure parser function. No explicit real subprocess/OS requirement in this behavior text (a=no), no LLM output dependency (b=no), no multi-agent chain (c=no). PASS2 down-level Unit rejected because "distinguish ... failures from valid-markdown payloads" implies classification outcome across handling flow, not isolated function math. Up-level System rejected because behavior does not require "real process execution" (covered separately in 4/5).
behaviors.test_description: In one in-process suite, feed (A) valid JSON containing markdown-heavy strings and (B) truly malformed JSON; assert A takes success path and B emits parse-failure path/trace, with no markdown-only false positives.
